blocks:
Sūrat al-Nās

It is Madani (Medinan), and it is like Sūrat al-Falaq because it is one of the two protective surahs (المعوّذتين). It consists of six verses without dispute. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ. (1) Say, 'I seek the protection of the Lord of humans, (٢) مَلِكِ النَّاسِ. (2) the King of humans, (٣) إِلهِ النَّاسِ. (3) the God of humans, (٤) مِنْ شَرِّ الْوَسْواسِ ** (4) from the evil of the sneaking whisperer, meaning the whisperer (الموسوس). He (Allah) expressed it using الوسواس (the whisper) for emphasis (مبالغة). الْخَنَّاسِ ** whose habit is to sneak away (يخنس), meaning to retreat (يتأخّر), when a person remembers his Lord. Al-Qummi said: الْخَنَّاسِ ** is the name of Satan (الشيطان). (٥) الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ ** (5) who whispers into the chests of humans, when they are heedless of the remembrance of their Lord. (٦) مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ ** (6) from among the jinn and humans.' This is an explanation (بيان) for الْوَسْواسِ.**

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: There is no believer except that his heart has two ears within his chest: one ear into which الْوَسْواسِ الْخَنَّاسِ ** breathes, and one ear into which the angel (الملك) breathes. So, Allah supports the believer through the angel. That is His saying: وَأَيَّدَهُمْ بِرُوحٍ مِنْهُ (and strengthened them with a spirit from Him). And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): There is no heart except that it has two ears: upon one of them is a guiding angel (ملك مرشد), and upon the other is a tempting devil (شيطان مفتن). This one commands him, and that one restrains him. Likewise, there is a devil (شيطان) from among humans who incites people to commit sins, just as the devil from the jinn incites them. The explanation of the devils among humans (شياطين الإنس) has already passed in Sūrat al-An'am, and the reason for the revelation of this surah and the reward for its recitation preceded in the commentary on its sister surah.
